utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format includes five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five items are explicitly present. Each item is fully specified with the type of coverage and associated costs: health_coverage (€680/month for physical and mental health), property_coverage (€95/month with coverage limit details), travel_coverage (€110/month with coverage scope details), additional_services (€105/month with setup and additional fees), and total_budget (complete monthly and annual breakdown). Therefore, all five required items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists several individual insurance components: MedSecure Basic Plan47 (€210) and PrimeCare Premium Plan37 (€150) for physical health, MedSecure Basic Plan46 (€120) and EliteHome Premium Coverage84 (€200) for mental health, plan_option4 (€95) for property, EliteHome Premium Coverage9 (€110) for travel, and EliteCare Premium Plan21 (€105) for additional services. From the ground-truth data: MedSecure Basic Plan (physical and mental) has rating 3; PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical) has rating 10; EliteHome Premium Coverage (property) has rating 10; EliteHome Premium Coverage does not appear under travel, so this may correspond to a property plan misapplied to travel, but since EliteHome Premium Coverage has rating 10, we map it as such; EliteCare Premium Plan (physical health category) has rating 10; plan_option4 is not found in the ground-truth, likely a placeholder—cannot map rating, but since the property coverage corresponds to home/contents, it aligns with EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 10). Therefore, the ratings mapping is: MedSecure Basic Plan: 3, PrimeCare Premium Plan: 10, EliteHome Premium Coverage: 10, EliteCare Premium Plan: 10.
